title: Before anything existed, there was God.
text:
Before anything existed, there was God.
If nothing had existed, there would still be God.

I exist, and that keeps me noisy.
If I had not existed, what would have been lost?

title: I carried a thousand desires, each one lethal.
text:
I carried a thousand desires, each one lethal.
Most of them still survived me.

One wish after another failed to kill me cleanly.
What finished me was the waiting.

title: This heart was never made for obedience.
text:
This heart was never made for obedience.
It kneels only where it breaks.

I tried to train it into peace.
It kept returning to your name.

title: Love made me poor in every useful way.
text:
Love made me poor in every useful way.
I can no longer pass for practical.

What remains in me is not competence.
It is a lit room no one can sleep in.

title: Ask the wound what survives after the blade.
text:
Ask the wound what survives after the blade.
Ask the scar what survives after the wound.

What lasts is memory in a harsher form:
a body that keeps a record against its will.

title: Faith is not a blanket, it is a fire alarm.
text:
Faith is not a blanket, it is a fire alarm.
It does not soothe me; it wakes me.

Every answer I hold turns porous.
Every night the sky asks again.

title: I rehearsed my death until it felt like craft.
text:
I rehearsed my death until it felt like craft.
I learned posture, silence, and exit.

Living stayed the difficult art:
improvised, public, unfinished.

title: The cup returns because thirst remembers.
text:
The cup returns because thirst remembers.
I keep refusing, and still I drink.

Habit is stronger than resolve.
Night is stronger than habit.

title: The mirror lends me a face and sends me the bill.
text:
The mirror lends me a face and sends me the bill.
Identity is a rented room.

By dawn, even my reflection asks:
who exactly was living here?

title: What you call clarity is often fear in clean clothes.
text:
What you call clarity is often fear in clean clothes.
I trust the fog more than certainty.

Fog admits it cannot see far.
Certainty lies and calls it wisdom.

title: I had thousands of desires, each fierce enough to take my life.
text:
I had thousands of desires, each fierce enough to take my life.
Many were fulfilled, yet they still felt too few.

I cannot complain that the world gave me nothing.
I complain that what it gave arrived like debt.

Life slipped by while I negotiated with longing.
Each bargain cost more than the thing I won.

My heart did not die from one grand catastrophe.
It wore out under minor, daily wreckage.

If grief had shape, I could have wrestled it.
Instead it came as weather and entered everything.

People call this endurance; I call it delay.
Death keeps missing appointments and sending flowers.

I smiled enough to be called resilient.
Inside, I kept inventory of losses.

By the end, desire was not hope.
It was the habit of reaching into emptiness.

title: Naive heart, what has happened to you?
text:
Naive heart, what has happened to you?
What is this pain that has no diagnosis?

We both know there is no remedy,
yet we return to the same physician.

You call this love; I call it recurrence.
The wound has learned my schedule.

Night after night, you reopen old doors
as if memory were a country we can re-enter.

We ask for calm and provoke the storm.
We ask for distance and dial her name.

Even prayer becomes a form of pursuit.
Even silence turns into argument.

My heart, if this is your chosen ruin,
at least admit you chose it knowingly.

Still, when her shadow crosses the mind,
all my wisdom resigns at once.

title: It was never in my fate to meet you.
text:
It was never in my fate to meet you.
Had I lived longer, this waiting would still remain.

Your promise of union was elegant,
but elegance is not evidence.

I burned with desire for one impossible scene:
you and I in the same room, without metaphor.

Time did what time always does:
it turned urgency into ritual.

Letters arrived with perfume and delay.
Each sentence carried both nectar and refusal.

I learned to survive on almost.
Almost became my native tongue.

Friends said, forget her.
As if the mind were a switch and not a city.

If fate denied meeting, it granted this much:
I became exact in longing.

Call it failure if you like.
I call it a life spent facing one direction.

title: No hope bears fruit, no shape of relief appears.
text:
No hope bears fruit, no shape of relief appears.
Why does night keep returning to my house?

I do not die, yet I do not live cleanly.
I occupy a corridor between verdicts.

The world asks me to be reasonable.
Reason arrives late and leaves early.

I write to her and tear up the page.
I tear up the page and still send it in my blood.

Every sound becomes a possible footstep.
Every footstep passes by.

People praise patience from a safe distance.
Patience from inside feels like rust.

My body remains; my center has migrated.
I speak, but the voice comes from elsewhere.

If this is healing, it is strangely cruel.
If this is ruin, it is strangely orderly.

Dawn offers no revelation.
Only another shift in the same factory of ache.

title: Every task feels impossible, and calling it easy is hardest of all.
text:
Every task feels impossible, and calling it easy is hardest of all.
To become human itself is no small work.

What looks simple from outside
is a war when lived from within.

Love asks for surrender without map.
Ego asks for victory without battle.

I tried to remain balanced.
The heart chose extremity.

One glance from her, and all my systems crash.
Then I pretend composure for the crowd.

People worship outcomes.
I have studied process: mostly smoke.

Even prayer needs stamina.
Even sin requires organization.

I am tired of being advised.
Advice cannot carry weight for me.

Do not call this pessimism.
Call it precise reporting from the front.

title: I do not call this love; call it obsession if you prefer.
text:
I do not call this love; call it obsession if you prefer.
Names do not alter the fever.

If she ignores me, I intensify.
If she appears, I destabilize.

The wise recommend moderation.
Desire laughs at policy.

I make vows in the morning
and break them before noon.

Her cruelty is not theatrical.
It is efficient, almost administrative.

I say I am done.
Then one remembered gesture undoes the statement.

Let jurists debate morality.
I am busy surviving contact with beauty.

This is not romance in polished script.
This is addiction with spiritual vocabulary.

Still, if she called once,
I would arrive before the echo.

title: At the beginning of love, why cry as if this is the end?
text:
At the beginning of love, why cry as if this is the end?
There is much more to happen.

The first wound is only orientation.
You have not yet seen the map of fire.

Today you fear one unanswered message.
Tomorrow you will fear your own silence.

Desire starts as sweetness.
Then it learns ambition.

You think union is the goal.
Soon you will discover depth is the cost.

Every joy in love carries a hidden blade.
Every blade leaves a brighter edge.

Do not ask for safety.
Ask for capacity.

If you must enter, enter fully.
Half-measures rot faster than grief.

What comes will remake your language.
You will speak in weather, glass, and ash.

title: A sigh needs a lifetime to arrive at impact.
text:
A sigh needs a lifetime to arrive at impact.
Who rushes grief misunderstands physics.

I exhaled once, years ago.
Only now does the world begin to tremble.

Prayer is not instant messaging.
It is seed, winter, and patience.

The heart sends signals through stone.
Stone replies on geological time.

I kept asking for a sign.
The sign was delay itself.

What burns quickly also cools quickly.
I wanted a flame that could survive weather.

So I learned the slow disciplines:
breathing through loss, naming without owning.

When relief finally arrived,
it looked exactly like endurance.

A sigh, then, is not weakness.
It is architecture built out of breath.

title: This world is a children's playground before me.
text:
This world is a children's playground before me.
Night and day are just a spectacle.

People panic over gain and loss.
I watch the drama, unconvinced.

Crowns, uniforms, signatures, seals:
props in a traveling show.

What they call permanence
changes shift by shift.

I have seen empires speak in thunder
and disappear in paperwork.

I have seen saints bargain,
and sinners illuminate a room.

So forgive my laughter at your certainties.
I have audited too many illusions.

If I seem detached,
it is because I have loved deeply enough to see through display.

The Real remains unadvertised.
Everything else is stagecraft.

title: Love made me what I am: a city after bombardment.
text:
Love made me what I am: a city after bombardment.
Streets intact, addresses erased.

From outside I look inhabitable.
Inside, whole districts are ash.

I host conversations gracefully
while collapse keeps spreading underground.

No one sees the emergency lights.
No one hears the structural groan.

I was not ruined in one dramatic hour.
I was ruined by repeated small impacts.

Your absence became a municipal policy.
Everything closed early.

Even joy had to apply for clearance.
Most requests were denied.

And yet, in one forgotten lane,
a single lamp still refuses extinction.

Call that foolishness, or devotion.
I call it the last unoccupied room of the heart.
